text: First pregnancy in a 21-year-old, estimated at approximately 9-10 weeks gestation by last menstrual period (mid-January)
transcript_quote: First pregnancy. Twenty-one years old, first everything.

text: Patient reports missed period followed by two home pregnancy tests both positive two weeks ago
transcript_quote: Two tests. Both positive, like, instantly. That was two weeks ago

text: In-office pregnancy test performed and confirmed positive
transcript_quote: We've already confirmed your pregnancy test here this morning.

text: Patient reports significant anxiety/nervousness related to first pregnancy and everyday chronic stress
transcript_quote: I've been alternating between browsing baby names and completely freaking out ever since.

text: Chronic neck and low back pain since falling off a horse (barrel racing) two summers ago; pain severity fluctuates from background to incapacitating
transcript_quote: Two summers ago I came off hard, like really hard, and my neck and lower back have never been the same since.

text: Patient currently taking hydrocodone-acetaminophen 5 mg tablets for severe pain days, most days recently

slots.freq: as needed for bad days, most days lately

transcript_quote: the hydrocodone with acetaminophen — the five milligram tablets

text: Patient currently taking tramadol 50 mg for moderate pain days
transcript_quote: tramadol, fifty milligrams

text: Counseled patient not to stop opioids suddenly due to risk of withdrawal, which is dangerous to mother and baby in pregnancy
transcript_quote: do not stop these medicines suddenly. Your body is used to them, and stopping cold turkey puts you into withdrawal — and withdrawal is genuinely risky in pregnancy

text: Plan for careful, gradual opioid taper coordinated with pain specialist; patient to continue current doses (least needed to function) until written schedule provided
transcript_quote: the plan is a careful, gradual taper, and I don't want you improvising it alone... Until it's in place, keep to your current doses

text: Counseled that continued opioid use through pregnancy risks neonatal withdrawal (treatable but difficult)
transcript_quote: the main one is that the baby can be born needing to withdraw, which is treatable but hard on everyone

text: Referral/coordination planned with patient's pain specialist to build step-down taper schedule this week
transcript_quote: I'll reach out to your pain specialist this week so we're building the step-down schedule together

text: Recommended non-opioid pain management: pregnancy-safe physical therapy, heat, stretching, pacing, positioning
transcript_quote: physical therapy that's safe in pregnancy, heat, stretching, pacing your days, positioning tricks as your body changes

text: No vaginal bleeding or spotting
transcript_quote: No bleeding, no real cramping.

text: No significant cramping; mild low pulling twinges reported
transcript_quote: Twinges? Like little pulling feelings low down, but nothing scary.

text: Nausea, mostly mornings, with occasional vomiting
transcript_quote: So much nausea. Mornings mostly. I've actually thrown up a few times

text: No dysuria, no fevers
transcript_quote: Any burning when you pee, fevers, anything like that? Nope.

text: Urinary frequency reported without dysuria
transcript_quote: Pee is frequent but polite.

text: No new headaches or vision changes; only chronic neck-related headaches
transcript_quote: Just my normal neck-headaches. Nothing new or weird.

text: Never smoked; one glass of wine before pregnancy known, no alcohol since; no other substances besides prescribed pain medications

slots.alcohol: one glass before knowing, none since

transcript_quote: Never smoked. I had, like, one glass of wine at a birthday before I knew — nothing since we found out.

text: Patient counseled to stop riding/barrel racing (saddle time) due to fall risk in pregnancy; ground time with horses permitted
transcript_quote: Ground time with the horses is absolutely fine. Saddle time needs to pause.

text: Patient not currently working due to chronic pain; recently completed degree
transcript_quote: It's actually why I'm not working right now. I finished my degree and then the pain just made every job I tried miserable.

text: Obstetric ultrasound for fetal viability performed: intrauterine pregnancy confirmed, fetal heartbeat present, size consistent with dates, normal healthy early pregnancy

slots.findings: intrauterine, cardiac activity present, size matches dates

transcript_quote: everything is sitting right where it should be — this is a normal, healthy early pregnancy, and the size matches your dates nicely

text: Physical examination performed
transcript_quote: I'll do a physical exam

text: Uterine fundal height evaluation performed
transcript_quote: measure your uterus from the outside

text: Auscultation of fetal heart with Doppler performed
transcript_quote: listen with the Doppler

text: Blood group typing ordered/performed
transcript_quote: blood type

text: Complete blood count (hemogram) ordered/performed
transcript_quote: a complete blood count

text: Hepatitis B surface antigen screening ordered/performed
transcript_quote: screening for hepatitis B

text: Hepatitis C antibody screening ordered/performed
transcript_quote: hepatitis B and C

text: HIV screening ordered/performed
transcript_quote: HIV

text: Syphilis screening ordered/performed
transcript_quote: and syphilis

text: Chlamydia swab/test ordered/performed
transcript_quote: swabs for chlamydia and gonorrhea

text: Gonorrhea swab/test ordered/performed
transcript_quote: swabs for chlamydia and gonorrhea

text: Rubella immunity screening ordered/performed
transcript_quote: immunity checks for rubella and chickenpox

text: Varicella (chickenpox) immunity screening ordered/performed
transcript_quote: immunity checks for rubella and chickenpox

text: Pap smear (cervical cytology) performed
transcript_quote: a Pap smear during the exam

text: Urine culture ordered/performed
transcript_quote: a urine culture

text: Urine qualitative protein check ordered/performed
transcript_quote: a urine protein check

text: Urine glucose/diabetes screening ordered/performed
transcript_quote: a urine sugar screen

text: Tuberculosis skin test (Tine) placed on forearm, to be read in a couple of days
transcript_quote: a little TB skin test on your forearm that we look at again in a couple of days

text: Nausea management counseling: small frequent snacks, fluids between meals, and call clinic if unable to keep anything down
transcript_quote: small frequent snacks, fluids between meals instead of with them, and if you get to where you can't keep anything down, you call us

text: Counseled that everyday stress is not dangerous to the baby; stress will be monitored each visit with referral to support resources if it escalates
transcript_quote: we'll check in on it at every visit, and if it climbs, we have people to connect you with

text: Lifestyle counseling: walking, gentle stretching, adequate sleep
transcript_quote: walking, gentle stretching, actual sleep

text: Follow-up prenatal visits every four weeks, closer to term
transcript_quote: Visits about every four weeks for now, closer together toward the end.

text: Reassured patient that pre-recognition opioid use has not doomed the pregnancy
transcript_quote: you have not doomed this baby. What matters is what we do from here forward.
